units.content: physical measurement apparatus interacts with physical target to obtain at least one measurement record
acquired record is stored together with further measurement records in a storage
each stored record is a directed entry with six fields: producing measurer identifier, target entity identifier, property identifier, numerical value, method identifier, and source tag
source tag takes one of two predetermined values to distinguish physical-interaction records from computation-produced records
storage is append-only with composite-key overwrite and an external append-only event log
records are grouped by (target, property) pair
for each group with two or more records, a relative-spread measure of numerical values is computed
each group is classified as converged, tension, divergent, or single based on relative-spread measure
for each measurer, its groups in each classification are counted
each measurer is classified as systemic-crack, systemic-opening, local-crack, or none based on group classification counts
for each tension or divergent group, the distribution of numerical values is tested for Gaussian-mixture decomposition into two or more components
decomposition is subject to minimum-size, BIC-improvement, and minimum-separation guards
when decomposition succeeds, a split of the target entity identifier is proposed
the proposed split is effected on the storage
a machine-readable feedback signal is emitted encoding each measurer classification and each proposed split
feedback signal is routed to an actor configured to annotate storage or modify how subsequent records are acquired by the physical measurement apparatus
the method does not maintain an ontology layer assigning semantic types to entities, properties, or methods
the method does not maintain a separate object-type or property-type registry external to the storage
the method does not maintain a separate model-card, model-registry, or model-configuration record external to storage for characterising a producing measurer
any characterising attribute of a producing measurer is represented as a directed entry in storage under the same six-field schema as a measurement record of a physical target
